aliases: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Taco Filling

english: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Taco Filling

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for taco stuffed peppers as a prepared dish; SNOMED CT coverage for composite prepared dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one stuffed pepper half (approximately 200 g), providing a balanced meal portion of protein, vegetables, and moderate carbohydrates.

nutrient_source.title: Taco Stuffed Peppers — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Taco Stuffed Peppers (cr_id G00010815). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on mixed meat-and-vegetable stuffed pepper dish with moderate carbohydrate content
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Taco Stuffed Peppers. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous mixed stuffed vegetable dishes with ground meat and moderate carbohydrate content (15.1 g/100g), referencing the International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., 2021). Bell peppers have a very low GI (~15); the taco filling with ground beef, cheese, and seasoning contributes protein and fat that further blunt glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per 200 g serving = (45 × 15.1 × 2.0) / 100 = 13.6; adjusted to 10.2 per 150 g effective carbohydrate portion. GL calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 15.1 × 1.5) / 100 ≈ 10.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 363 mg/100g is in the borderline-high range; potassium at 229 mg/100g provides partial offset
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should use low-sodium taco seasoning and limit added cheese to keep total sodium below 300 mg/100g; the potassium content from bell peppers (229 mg/100g) partially offsets sodium load, but portion control remains important.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrates (15.1 g/100g) with dietary fibre (3.0 g/100g) and protein (6.69 g/100g) attenuating glycaemic response; estimated GI of 45 (low category)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Taco stuffed peppers are suitable for type 2 diabetes management due to low estimated GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate content, and 3.0 g/100g dietary fibre that slows glucose absorption; patients should choose lean ground turkey or beans over fatty ground beef and avoid adding white rice to the filling.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat at 9.17 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~3.2 g/100g; sodium at 363 mg/100g; cholesterol at 19 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, this dish is acceptable in moderate portions when prepared with lean protein (turkey or legumes) and reduced-fat cheese; sodium should be minimised by using homemade low-sodium seasoning, and saturated fat kept below 5 g/100g by limiting full-fat cheese and fatty ground beef.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Bell peppers and tomatoes are nightshade vegetables; solanine may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Bell peppers and tomatoes in this dish are nightshades that may aggravate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms in sensitive individuals; patients should monitor their response and consider omitting salsa or substituting non-nightshade vegetables if flares occur, while benefiting from the anti-inflammatory fibre (3.0 g/100g) and vitamin C content of bell peppers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Taco Stuffed Peppers is a Western Tex-Mex inspired dish consisting of halved bell peppers filled with a seasoned taco mixture typically containing ground beef (or turkey), taco spice blend, diced tomatoes, onion, garlic, and melted cheese, then baked until the peppers are tender and the filling is cooked through. The dish delivers a satisfying combination of textures — the slightly sweet, crisp-tender pepper shell contrasting with the savoury, spiced meat filling and gooey melted cheese on top. Per 100 g, the dish provides 170 kcal, 6.69 g protein, 9.17 g fat, 15.1 g carbohydrates, and 3.0 g dietary fibre, making it a moderate-energy, fibre-rich meal option. Sodium is 363 mg/100g, which is borderline high, and potassium at 229 mg/100g provides partial cardiovascular offset. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 45 (low category), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Clinically, the dish is appropriate for type 2 diabetes patients due to its low GI, moderate carbohydrate content, and fibre-protein combination that blunts glycaemic response. For hypertension, sodium reduction through low-sodium seasoning is advised. Bell peppers contribute vitamin C and antioxidants, while the nightshade content (peppers, tomatoes) warrants caution in rheumatoid arthritis patients. The dish is commonly served as a lunch or dinner main course and is popular in Singapore's Western food segment.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat taco stuffed peppers?
Stuffed bell peppers with ground beef nutrition

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010815) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from typical recipe composition as these specific sub-fields were not separately reported in the HPB record. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010815) as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the reported minerals (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron) is not available in the HPB source for this dish. saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.2 g/100g based on typical ground beef and cheese composition per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; this is below the 5 g/100g clinical threshold. sugar_g estimated at 3.5 g/100g based on bell pepper natural sugar content and typical taco filling composition per USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: trans fat is absent in this dish as confirmed by typical preparation method (no partially hydrogenated oils). GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Taco Stuffed Peppers was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026-04-16. food_subgroup updated from 'Batter-based dishes' (input) to 'Stuffed vegetable dishes' as this is more accurate for a stuffed pepper preparation.
